titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Oliver Ellsworth Huntington Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Stephanie Bredbenner

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday June 19, 2026 at 03:04

did.unittitle: Oliver Ellsworth Huntington family papers

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred to Manuscripts and Archives from the Yale Divinity School Library, before 2010.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material is arranged in rough chronological order, with undated materials filed at the end.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Oliver Ellsworth Huntington (1802-1877) was a pharmacist and merchant in Connecticut, New York, and Ohio. He was born in Norwich, Connecticut to Joseph Huntington (1768-1837) and Eunice Carew Huntington (1769-1848). Huntington graduated from Yale College in 1825 and went into business with his partner William E. Dodge (1805-1883) in New York. He later moved to Ohio and was active in local politics, commerce, and religious activities. Oliver Ellsworth Huntington married his first wife, Mary Ann Strong Huntington (1806-1840) in 1830 and his second wife, Eunice Kimberly Hitchcock Huntington (1825-1910) in 1854. He had four children with his first wife. His son, Henry Strong Huntington, also attended Yale and became a Congregational minister. Oliver Ellsworth Huntington died in 1877 in Ohio.

The Huntington family descends from Simon Huntington (1583-1633) and Margaret Barrett Huntington (circa 1590s-1665 or 1666). Prominent members of the family include Samuel Huntington (1731-1796), the first governor of Connecticut; Jabez Huntington (1719-1786) (Yale 1741), merchant and Connecticut politician; and Ellsworth Huntington (1876-1947), a professor of geography at Yale and president of the Ecological Society of America, Association of American Geographers, and the American Eugenics Society.

Biographical information in this note is primarily drawn from The Huntington Family in America: A Genealogical Memoir of the Known Descendants of Simon Huntington from 1633 to 1915 (Hartford, Connecticut: Huntington Family Association, 1915).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Oliver Ellsworth Huntington Family Papers (MS 2136). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was put in its current arrangement during processing in 2023. All folder titles have been supplied by the archivist. In cases where there are multiple members of the Huntington family with the same name, birth and death dates are provided in the folder titles.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related materials: Ellsworth Huntington papers (MS 1); and Strong family collection (MS 1198).

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated materials: Henry S. Huntington family papers (RG 109), Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the life and family of Oliver Ellsworth Huntington, particularly life in Norwich, Connecticut, his time as a student at Yale College, and his career as a pharmacist and merchant in New York, Connecticut, and Ohio. Included are Huntington's commonplace book, Yale College disputations, indentures, business records, and correspondence. Also present are deeds, indentures, correspondence, and other legal and financial records related to other members of the Huntington family, including Oliver Ellsworth Huntington's father Joseph Huntington (1768-1837); grandfather Andrew Huntington (1745-1824); grandfather Joseph Carew (1738-1818); Jedediah Huntington (1743-1818); Jonathan Huntington (1696-1773); Joseph Huntington (1661-1747); Joshua Huntington (1751-1821); Roger Huntington (1757-1835); Roswell Huntington (1763-1836); Governor Samuel Huntington (1731-1796); Jabez Williams Huntington (1788-1847); Ebenezer Devotion (1740-1829); and Eunice Huntington Devotion (1742-1827). The collection also contains an account book, political petitions, newspaper clippings, printed material, and other documents concerning social, political, and religious life in Norwich, Connecticut. A small quantity of twentieth century material relates to Oliver Ellsworth Huntington's grandson Ellsworth Huntington, primarily correspondence and newspaper clippings.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Oliver Ellsworth Huntington are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on the use of these materials. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Deed concerning Joseph Huntington (1661-1747)
Deeds and legal documents concerning Jonathan Huntington (1695-1773)
Fragments of Jonathan Huntington's eulogy
Financial document concerning Joshua Huntington (1751-1821)
Letter and receipt concerning Jedediah Huntington and Andrew Huntington (1745-1824)
Document with Governor Samuel Huntington's signature and accompanying letter to Ellsworth Huntington
Ship invoice concerning Andrew Huntington (1745-1824)
Legal and financial documents concerning Governor Samuel Huntington
Legal and financial documents concerning Captain Joseph Carew
Financial document concerning Roswell Huntington (1763-1836)
Deed concerning Roger Huntington (1757-1835), Ebenezer Devotion (1740-1829), and Eunice Huntington Devotion (1742-1827)
Legal and financial documents concerning Carew & Huntington
Legal and financial documents concerning Joseph Huntington (1768-1837)
Other financial documents
The Progress of Dulness by John Trumbull
Copies of George Braikenridge letters to Francis Jerdone
Members of Mr. Strong's church, First Society of Norwich
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington correspondence
Connecticut petitions
Copies of poems and songs
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington commonplace book
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington Yale College disputations
Andover Theological Seminary catalogs
Lancasterian Juvenile Elocution Society exhibition broadside
Phillips Academy Andover catalog
First Ecclesiastical Society in Norwich resolution
Draft of a petition to the United States Postmaster General concerning the renaming of the Norwich post office
Account book
Huntington & Dodge financial records
Norwich Anti-swearing Society constitution
Young Men's Meeting in Behalf of the Cherokees invitation
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington and William E. Dodge indenture
Jabez Williams Huntington letter to Mr. and Mrs. W. Williams
Letters to George Washington Gale and Jonathan Blanchard
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington financial documents
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington letters to newspaper editors
Newspaper clippings
Cuyahoga County Bible Society records
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington letter to the editor of The Daily True Democrat concerning the Hartford Convention
Notes on ministry and sermons
Oliver Ellsworth Huntington correspondence and ephemera concerning Yale College
Notes concerning chemistry, theology, and tobacco
G. Grant letter
Norwich bicentennial invitation and newspaper clipping
Conversation with Levi Flanders during his severe sickness
Notes concerning the Civil War

title.part: Catalogue of the Alpha Delta Phi, 1832-1915

did.unittitle: Ellsworth Huntington correspondence
Newspaper clippings concerning Huntington family members
Military orders to Captain Ellis
Notes on peerage and nobility in England
Genealogical notes on the Huntington family
Unidentified fragments
Oversize newspaper clippings
